statement.markdown: Solve the problem and fix the bugs.
statement.inputFormat:
n (integer) as array size
nums (array of integers)
statement.outputFormat: true or false whether target exists in nums
statement.notes: Time complexity: O(log n), Space complexity: O(1)

hints.text: Think about what the code is trying to do and how to correctly implement the binary search algorithm.

editorial.title: Editorial Title
editorial.intuition: The binary search algorithm works by dividing the search space in half with each iteration until the target is found or the space is exhausted.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force approach: O(n) time complexity, where n is the size of the array.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution should use binary search to find the target in O(log n) time complexity.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The correctness proof involves showing that the function returns true if the target is in the array and false otherwise.

functionSignature.java: public boolean search(int[] nums, int target);
functionSignature.python: def search(self, nums: list[int], target: int) -> bool:
